name: Major Muscles and Their Actions
description: The major skeletal muscles you can name and feel, grouped head to toe, with the main movement each one produces. Extras give location and a memorable role for muscles like the masseter, latissimus dorsi, and sartorius.

fields.front: Which muscle clenches the jaw and is one of the strongest muscles by force?
fields.extra: One of the muscles of chewing; you can feel it bulge at the angle of the jaw when you bite down.

fields.front: Which fan-shaped muscle on the side of the head helps close the jaw?
fields.extra: A chewing muscle that also draws the lower jaw backward.

fields.front: Which ring of muscle around the eye closes the eyelids?
fields.extra: It powers blinking, winking, and squinting.

fields.front: Which muscle purses the lips, used in whistling and kissing?
fields.extra: The ring of muscle encircling the mouth.

fields.front: Which muscle lifts the corners of the mouth into a smile?
fields.extra: It runs from the cheekbone to the corner of the mouth; the 'smiling muscle'.

fields.front: Which paired neck muscle turns and bends the head?
fields.extra: Runs from the breastbone and collarbone to behind the ear; turning your head makes the opposite one stand out.

fields.front: Which large diamond-shaped upper-back muscle shrugs and steadies the shoulders?
fields.extra: It raises, lowers, and pulls back the shoulder blades and extends the neck; it spans from the skull to the mid-back.

fields.front: Which muscle caps the shoulder and lifts the arm out to the side?
fields.extra: The main muscle that raises the arm sideways; a common site for injections.

fields.front: Which broad back muscle pulls the arm downward and backward?
fields.extra: The 'swimmer's muscle'; key in pull-ups, rowing, and swimming.

fields.front: Which large chest muscle draws the arm across the body?
fields.extra: The main muscle worked by push-ups and the bench press.

fields.front: Which muscles between the shoulder blades pull them toward the spine?
fields.extra: They square the shoulders; weakness leads to a slouched posture.

fields.front: Which upper-arm muscle bends the elbow and turns the palm up?
fields.extra: It has two heads and bulges when you 'make a muscle'.

fields.front: Which muscle on the back of the upper arm straightens the elbow?
fields.extra: It has three heads and opposes the biceps; it does the pushing work.

fields.front: Which forearm muscle bends the elbow, especially with the thumb pointing up?
fields.extra: Stands out during a hammer curl or when carrying with a neutral grip.

fields.front: Which muscle bends the trunk forward and forms the 'six-pack'?
fields.extra: The six-pack look comes from bands of connective tissue crossing the muscle.

fields.front: Which abdominal muscles let you twist and bend to the side?
fields.extra: They run diagonally along the sides of the abdomen.

fields.front: Which deep abdominal muscle wraps the waist like a natural corset?
fields.extra: The deepest belly muscle; it compresses the abdomen and braces the spine.

fields.front: Which group of muscles runs beside the spine to keep you upright?
fields.extra: A column on each side of the backbone; it straightens the back and holds posture.

fields.front: Which dome-shaped muscle is the main muscle of breathing?
fields.extra: It flattens as it contracts to pull air into the lungs; it divides the chest from the abdomen.

fields.front: Which is the largest muscle in the body, driving hip extension?
fields.extra: The main muscle of the buttock; it powers standing up, climbing, and sprinting.

fields.front: Which hip muscle steadies the pelvis when you stand on one leg?
fields.extra: If it is weak, the pelvis drops on the opposite side with each step.

fields.front: Which deep muscle is the most powerful flexor of the hip?
fields.extra: It lifts the thigh toward the trunk and links the lower spine and pelvis to the femur.

fields.front: Which four-part muscle group on the front of the thigh straightens the knee?
fields.extra: The knee-jerk reflex tests it; it includes the rectus femoris and three vastus muscles.

fields.front: Which muscle group at the back of the thigh bends the knee and extends the hip?
fields.extra: Three muscles; commonly pulled in sprinting and jumping.

fields.front: Which is the longest muscle in the body, crossing the thigh diagonally?
fields.extra: It runs from the hip to the inner knee and helps you sit cross-legged (the 'tailor's muscle').

fields.front: Which inner-thigh muscles pull the legs together?
fields.extra: Important for squeezing the legs together, as in horseback riding and skating.

fields.front: Which two-headed calf muscle lets you stand on tiptoe and push off when walking?
fields.extra: It forms the visible bulge of the calf and joins the Achilles tendon.

fields.front: Which deep calf muscle works with the gastrocnemius to point the foot and hold you upright?
fields.extra: It lies beneath the gastrocnemius and is vital for standing and walking.

fields.front: Which shin muscle lifts the foot upward?
fields.extra: It runs down the front of the shin; if it fails, the foot drags ('foot drop').

fields.front: What is the Achilles tendon, and what does it join?
fields.back: The strongest tendon in the body; it links the calf muscles to the heel bone
fields.extra: Not a muscle but a tendon. Rupturing it makes it impossible to push off with the toes.

fields.front: About how many skeletal muscles does the human body have?
fields.extra: Together they make up roughly 40% of body weight.

fields.front: What is the smallest skeletal muscle in the body?
fields.back: The stapedius, in the middle ear
fields.extra: About a millimeter long; it braces a tiny ear bone to dampen loud sounds.

fields.front: Which muscles are among the busiest, moving over 100,000 times a day?
fields.back: The muscles that move the eyes
fields.extra: They make constant tiny adjustments even when you think your gaze is still.
